content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string and wants to cut it into substrings such that every substring is a palindrome. Chef wants to minimize the number of cuts needed to achieve this. Help Chef find the minimum number of cuts required so that each part of the partitioned string is a palindrome.

Chef needs your help to write a function that, given the input string, returns the smallest number of cuts needed to partition the string into palindromic substrings.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMinimumCutsfindMinimumCutsfindMinimumCuts - This function calculates the minimum number of cuts needed to partition a given text into palindromic substrings.

Parameters
inputStringinputStringinputString (string): The text to be partitioned into palindromic substrings. It consists of lowercase English letters only.
Return Value
Returns an integer integerResultintegerResultintegerResult representing the minimum cuts required.
The output is a non-negative integer indicating the smallest number of cuts needed.
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains a string inputStringinputStringinputString representing Chef's text.
Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the minimum number of cuts required to partition the given string into palindromic substrings.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤∣inputString∣≤20001 \leq |inputString| \leq 20001≤∣inputString∣≤2000
Each inputStringinputStringinputString consists of lowercase English letters only.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
t=1: "a" is already a palindrome, so 0 cuts needed.
t=2: "racecar" is a palindrome, so 0 cuts needed.
t=3: "aabbbaa" already a palindrome, so 0 cuts needed.
t=4: "abcde" has no palindromic substrings longer than 1, so 4 cuts needed to isolate each character.

Explanation:
For "abba": The entire string is already a palindrome, so no cuts are needed. Output is 0.
For "banana": Minimum cuts are 1, splitting as "b" | "anana" where "anana" is a palindrome.
For "abcba": The whole string is a palindrome itself, so zero cuts are required. Output is 0.

solution.timeComplexity:
Palindrome precomputation: O(n²)
DP computation: O(n²)
Total: O(n²)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n²) for the palindrome table and dp array
